Cooldown

Momentum Factor

Idle Band — how far the temperature can drift from the target before the system activates. Wider = fewer cycles, more drift. Narrower = tighter control, more cycles. Min Run Time — the heater or AC must stay on for at least this long before switching off, even if the temperature is back in range. Prevents rapid short cycles that wear out equipment. Cooldown — minimum wait time after a heater or AC run before it can start again. Protects the appliance from quick restarts. Momentum & Thermal — control how early the system shuts off to account for heat still in the air. Auto-set from outdoor conditions each time weather refreshes, but you can fine-tune them here.
